title: 60 days to launch: The realistic timeline for building your no-code application
subheading: From idea to paying customers in 8 weeks using Bubble.io and strategic no-code development
excerpt: Learn how to build and launch your MVP in just 60 days using no-code tools. Complete timeline, budget breakdown, and proven process from idea to live product.
seoDescription: Learn how to build and launch your MVP in just 60 days using no-code tools. Complete timeline, budget breakdown, and proven process from idea to live product.
coverAlt: 60-day MVP development timeline workspace showing no-code development process with Bubble.io interface and project planning materials

toc.text: The App Launch Blueprint: your pre-build foundation

bodyHtml:
TL;DR

  • 60 days is our proven timeline for building a complete no-code MVP using Bubble.io
  • Timeline is flexible - can be faster for simple apps or extended for complex features
  • App Launch Blueprint precedes development to validate your concept and map everything out
  • All design and development happens directly in Bubble - no external wireframing tools
  • Typical budget ranges €3,000-€20,000 depending on complexity and features included

Why 60 days works (but it's flexible)

Traditional coding projects drag on for months. I have a friend whose dev team was supposed to finish app inside 6 months, it's now 3rd year and MVP is still not live.

We've cracked the code on delivering functional MVPs in exactly 60 days using Bubble.io and a focused approach.

But here's the thing - this isn't a rigid deadline. It's our optimal workflow that balances speed with quality. It can be also done faster or slower, depending on how many team members we hire on the project.

  • Faster timeline (30-45 days): Simple directory sites, basic CRUD apps, or when you skip certain processes like extensive user testing.
  • Standard timeline (60 days): Most SaaS platforms, marketplaces, and feature-rich applications with full testing and polish.
  • Extended timeline (75-90 days): Complex marketplaces, multi-platform launches, or when you want additional features beyond MVP scope.

Timeline affects pricing by roughly 15-25% in either direction. The key is being upfront about what you actually need.

Micro-takeaway: 60 days isn't arbitrary - it's the sweet spot where thorough planning meets rapid execution without cutting corners that matter.

The App Launch Blueprint: your pre-build foundation

Before any development starts, we create your App Launch Blueprint. This isn't billable development time - it's our strategic planning phase that prevents expensive mistakes later.

Your Blueprint includes:

  • Hero Screen Design: See exactly how your app will look when it's live. We design this directly in Bubble so you're seeing the real thing, not a mockup that might not translate properly.
  • User Flow Map: Every page, click, and interaction mapped out. No surprises during development about "how does a user get from A to B?"
  • Technical Roadmap: We identify potential technical challenges, required integrations (Stripe, APIs, etc.), and database structure before coding starts.
  • Strategic Project Analysis: We validate your concept against technical feasibility. Sometimes founders want features that sound simple but are technically complex - we catch this early.
  • Custom Investment Proposal: Transparent breakdown of exactly what you're paying for, timeline commitments, and what happens if scope changes.

This Blueprint phase typically takes 1-2 weeks and prevents the scope creep that kills timelines and budgets.

Micro-takeaway: The Blueprint isn't overhead - it's insurance against building the wrong thing or discovering deal-breaker issues mid-development.

👉 Want to start with your own custom Blueprint? Book a free strategy call here and we will meet to discuss your idea and start to prepare your Bluepring FOC (worth €800).

Week-by-week timeline breakdown

Weeks 1-2: Discovery & scoping

Goals: Lock down exactly what we're building and why.

We start with deep discovery call to understand your business model, target users, and success metrics. This isn't just feature gathering - we're identifying the core value proposition your MVP must deliver.

Key activities include documenting user stories, defining must-have vs. nice-to-have features, and competitive analysis. We use Notion to create a project wiki that becomes your single source of truth.

Reality check: Many founders come with 20+ features they "need." We help you identify the 3-5 that actually matter for validation.

Weeks 3-4: Database architecture & initial design

Goals: Set up the technical foundation and start building core screens.

Here's where we differ from traditional agencies - everything happens directly in Bubble. No Figma wireframes, no design handoffs, no "will this actually work in the platform?" surprises.

We build your database structure, set up user authentication, and create the core screens with real functionality. You can click through a working prototype by week 4, not just look at static designs.

Weeks 5-6: Core feature development

Goals: Build the primary functionality that delivers your value proposition.

This is where the magic happens. We implement your core workflows - user onboarding, the main feature set, and any automation that makes your app actually useful. Any required integrations (Stripe for payments, email services, APIs) get configured during this phase.

We work in one-week sprints with regular check-ins. You see progress constantly in your custom Client Portal, not just at the end.

AI Tip: Use ChatGPT to generate test data for your app during this phase. Having realistic content makes testing and demos much more compelling.

Week 7: Testing & refinement

Goals: Polish the user experience and fix any bugs.

We test every user flow methodically. This isn't just "does it work" - we're ensuring the experience is smooth enough that real users won't immediately bounce. Cross-device testing happens here too. Your app needs to work on phones, tablets, and desktops without breaking.

PRO tip: It's also a good idea to do your own team's Alpha testing to catch some edge-cases that we maybe didn't discuss before.

Week 8: Launch preparation & Go-Live

Goals: Deploy to production and hand over the keys.

Final deployment to your custom domain, switching integrations from test to live mode, setting up analytics tracking, and ensuring you know how to manage your app. We also handle any app store submissions if you're going mobile, though review times can add uncertainty.

Micro-takeaway: By week 8, you have a functioning business, not just an app. Users can sign up, pay you, and get value from day one.

Budget reality check

Typical range: €3,000 - €20,000 for a complete 60-day MVP build.

What drives the cost: Lower end (€3k-10k): Simple SaaS tools, directories, basic marketplaces. Fewer integrations, straightforward user flows. Higher end (€10k-20k): Complex marketplaces, multi-user platforms, heavy integration requirements, or when you want additional polish beyond MVP standards.

Cost factors that matter:
  • Number of user types (single user vs. buyer/seller marketplace)
  • Payment complexity (one-time vs. subscriptions vs. marketplace splits)
  • Required integrations (APIs, third-party services)
  • Custom design requirements beyond functional basics
What's included: Project management, database design, development, testing, launch support, and 2 weeks of post-launch bug fixes. What's not included: Ongoing maintenance, content creation, marketing site development, or feature additions beyond agreed scope.Micro-takeaway: At €8k average, you're paying roughly 15% of what traditional development costs while getting to market 5-8x faster.

Different MVP types, same process 

SaaS Platforms

Focus on the core workflow that delivers value. Billing integration is usually essential. Timeline holds well since it's typically single-user-type.

Marketplaces

More complex due to buyer/seller dynamics. May push toward the 75-day timeline or require launching with manual processes initially.

Directory/Listing Sites

Often the simplest builds. Can finish in 45 days if scope is tight. Perfect for testing market demand quickly.

Mobile Apps

We can build mobile-responsive web apps in Bubble, with a note that app store submission adds uncertainty to timeline and cost.

Micro-takeaway: The process adapts to your product type, but the discipline of rapid iteration and focused scope remains constant.

Your next steps

Ready to turn your idea into a live business in 60 days?

Step 1: Book a free strategy call with us. We will ask questions to understand your idea from all angles.Step 2: We'll map out your concept, identify potential challenges, and give you a realistic timeline and budget in custom App Launch Blueprint + design your first screen so you will see what to expect.Step 3: If everything aligns, we start building your app to get you launched within 60 days.

The founders who succeed with MVPs aren't the ones with the best ideas - they're the ones who execute fastest and learn from real users. 60 days from now, you could be welcoming your first paying customers.
